lines.line: # Founding of the Latinas and Power Symposium

> The speaker is the founder of the Latinas and Power Symposium, having created and produced the event. The symposium is in its 21st year and has exceeded expectations in terms of audience and the conversations it has sparked. The event continues to grow and attract attention, highlighting the speaker's significant role in its success.

metadata.title: Founding of the Latinas and Power Symposium
metadata.synopsis: The speaker is the founder of the Latinas and Power Symposium, having created and produced the event. The symposium is in its 21st year and has exceeded expectations in terms of audience and the conversations it has sparked. The event continues to grow and attract attention, highlighting the speaker's significant role in its success.

lines.line: # Family Roots and Puerto Rican Identity in Connecticut

> The speaker was born and raised in Connecticut, with parents who migrated from Puerto Rico in the early 1950s. The family's move to New Britain marked the establishment of the Puerto Rican community there. The speaker's father was entrepreneurial, and the family was deeply involved in civic engagement through the Puerto Rican Society Club of New Britain, which played a significant role in shaping the speaker's identity and commitment to community.

metadata.title: Family Roots and Puerto Rican Identity in Connecticut
metadata.synopsis: The speaker was born and raised in Connecticut, with parents who migrated from Puerto Rico in the early 1950s. The family's move to New Britain marked the establishment of the Puerto Rican community there. The speaker's father was entrepreneurial, and the family was deeply involved in civic engagement through the Puerto Rican Society Club of New Britain, which played a significant role in shaping the speaker's identity and commitment to community.

lines.line: # Legacy of Civic Engagement and Community Impact

> The speaker reflects on the legacy of their parents, particularly their father, who passed away seven years ago. The family's involvement in the Puerto Rican Society Club of New Britain is highlighted as a formative experience in civic engagement, which went against stereotypes and assumptions about Puerto Ricans. The speaker emphasizes the importance of community and the inclusive environment their family helped create.

metadata.title: Legacy of Civic Engagement and Community Impact

The speaker emphasizes the importance of community and the inclusive environment their family helped create.

lines.line: # Career Path and Empowering Latina Women

> The speaker discusses their career trajectory, including the challenges faced as a Latina in the corporate world and the lack of mentors. This led to the creation of the Latinas and Power Symposium, which began as a gathering of Latina professionals sharing strategies and has grown into a significant event for professional development and empowerment of Latina women.

metadata.title: Career Path and Empowering Latina Women
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses their career trajectory, including the challenges faced as a Latina in the corporate world and the lack of mentors. This led to the creation of the Latinas and Power Symposium, which began as a gathering of Latina professionals sharing strategies and has grown into a significant event for professional development and empowerment of Latina women.

lines.line: # Evolution of Latina Leadership

> The speaker describes the evolution of challenges and barriers for Latinas, from racism and discrimination to the struggle for representation in leadership roles. The Latinas and Power Symposium has become a platform for professional development, and the speaker's retirement from the corporate world has led to the creation of the Latinas in Leadership Institute (Lilly), which aims to build skills for leadership and advocacy.

metadata.title: Evolution of Latina Leadership
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes the evolution of challenges and barriers for Latinas, from racism and discrimination to the struggle for representation in leadership roles. The Latinas and Power Symposium has become a platform for professional development, and the speaker's retirement from the corporate world has led to the creation of the Latinas in Leadership Institute (Lilly), which aims to build skills for leadership and advocacy.

lines.line: # Collaboration Among Organizations

> The speaker acknowledges the existence of various organizations addressing the needs of Puerto Ricans and Latinos, both nationally and in Connecticut. They express a desire for more collaboration among these organizations to avoid operating in silos and to have a greater collective impact. The speaker also emphasizes the importance of recognizing and supporting the work of these organizations.

metadata.title: Collaboration Among Organizations
metadata.synopsis: The speaker acknowledges the existence of various organizations addressing the needs of Puerto Ricans and Latinos, both nationally and in Connecticut. They express a desire for more collaboration among these organizations to avoid operating in silos and to have a greater collective impact. The speaker also emphasizes the importance of recognizing and supporting the work of these organizations.

lines.line: # Mentoring and Civic Engagement

> The speaker discusses the importance of mentoring the next generation and the role of the Latinas in Leadership Institute in fostering new leaders. The institute's capstone projects aim to bring attention to issues affecting the Latino community and encourage young Latinas to engage in civic activities and advocacy.

metadata.title: Mentoring and Civic Engagement
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the importance of mentoring the next generation and the role of the Latinas in Leadership Institute in fostering new leaders. The institute's capstone projects aim to bring attention to issues affecting the Latino community and encourage young Latinas to engage in civic activities and advocacy.

lines.line: # Migration Patterns to Connecticut

> The speaker explains that Puerto Ricans continue to migrate to Connecticut due to established family roots and the opportunities that arose from agricultural work in the past. Despite the challenges of racism and discrimination, the Puerto Rican community has flourished in Connecticut, and the speaker emphasizes the state's significance as a place of opportunity and belonging.

metadata.title: Migration Patterns to Connecticut
metadata.synopsis: The speaker explains that Puerto Ricans continue to migrate to Connecticut due to established family roots and the opportunities that arose from agricultural work in the past. Despite the challenges of racism and discrimination, the Puerto Rican community has flourished in Connecticut, and the speaker emphasizes the state's significance as a place of opportunity and belonging.

lines.line: # Generational Shifts in Activism

> The speaker reflects on the changing nature of activism from the militant movements of the past to the more educated and articulate advocacy of today. They emphasize the importance of maintaining cultural identity and authenticity in leadership roles and the need for the Latino community to be more selective in choosing their representatives.

metadata.title: Generational Shifts in Activism
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the changing nature of activism from the militant movements of the past to the more educated and articulate advocacy of today. They emphasize the importance of maintaining cultural identity and authenticity in leadership roles and the need for the Latino community to be more selective in choosing their representatives.

lines.line: # Concluding Thoughts on Leadership

> In concluding the conversation, the speaker emphasizes the need for a long-term strategy to ensure the continuity of leadership and community engagement within the Puerto Rican community. They suggest creating a central database of community activities and a focused initiative to engage youth in understanding their cultural heritage and the importance of civic participation.

story.title: Marylin Alverlo
story.description: The transcript details an interview with Marilyn Alverio, discussing her work with the Latinas and Power Symposium, a platform she founded to empower Latina women through civic engagement and leadership development. Alverio outlines her personal journey from a Connecticut upbringing in a Puerto Rican community, highlighting the cultural and socio-economic challenges she faced. The discussion extends to her current initiatives aimed at addressing ongoing systemic barriers for Latinas, such as lack of representation and economic opportunities. Her efforts with educational programs and community organizing seek to inspire a new generation of Latina leaders, emphasizing the importance of cultural identity, civic engagement, and collaborative leadership to drive change.
